rationale: The transferable engine named in hyp_abstract is a three-part modeling toolkit — static generative link-placement rules, dynamical stability analysis via linearizing coupled equations and inspecting the Jacobian spectrum, and endogenous network growth through entry-plus-selection filtering that yields robust, self-organized structures. The candidate instead introduces a single stochastic wealth-exchange equation and derives a Pareto wealth distribution via a mean-field limit and a mapping to the directed-polymer problem; it contains no network-formation/selection process, no static link-generation rule, and no Jacobian/linear-stability treatment of coupled dynamics, so the machinery does not cohere. The object also differs genuinely: the query concerns ecological food-web structure and species-population dynamics, whereas the candidate concerns wealth distribution among economic agents via exchange/speculation, a different substrate and phenomenon even if both are loosely 'economy'-themed."
